text: The surgeon uses the left hand grasper to grasp the fatty tissue, creating tension that facilitates the dissection of the sides of the vessel wall.
The screen displays a directive to dissect through the fat layer to expose the vessel wall, with visible instructions at the top of the screen.
The surgeon uses scissors to scrape off residual fatty tissue from the anterior surface of the vessel without applying any energy, revealing the vessel wall beneath.
With the vessel now fully exposed, the surgeon prepares to proceed with bipolar coagulation, as indicated by the exercise prompt.
The surgeon uses a bipolar coagulation tool to grasp the vessel at two indicated points, applying energy until the targeted portion of the vessel visibly turns white.
The surgeon cuts the vessel in the middle of the two coagulation points after achieving satisfactory coagulation, as indicated by the exercise prompt.
